formatDefinitions.displayName: Complete with words from the box

exercises.title: A: Word Formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: The Wildlife Conservation Trust is an organisation (1) ...... (DEPEND) of state funding, dedicated to protecting endangered species and their natural habitats.
Its network of protected reserves spans the length and (2) ...... (BROAD) of the British Isles.
Since it was established in 1948, the Trust has had a (3) ...... (SIGNIFY) impact on conservation policy, contributing to the protection of hundreds of native species.
Because the Trust receives no government subsidy, it relies on the (4) ...... (GOOD) of individual supporters, funding its work through membership fees and (5) ...... (CHARITY) donations.
Critics have raised (6) ...... (OBJECT) to this approach, arguing that such an (7) ...... (VALUE) service to the environment ought to receive direct public funding.
The Trust's wardens are almost entirely volunteers, widely (8) ...... (KNOW) in their communities for their (9) ...... (EXAMPLE) dedication and (10) ...... (SELF) in protecting the natural world.

exercises.title: B: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

acceptedAnswers: has a lot in common with

fine I've got managing difficult customers ……………………………………………………. over the past few months.

dead Despite ……………………………………………………………… the company away-day, Gabriel went in the end.

Roberto should be made responsible for overseeing the accounts department.
charge It’s high time Roberto ………………………………………………………………… overseeing the accounts department.

My neighbours are furious with me for having noisy parties and have written to the landlord.
arms My neighbours, ……………………………………………………………… having noisy parties, have written to the landlord.

tale Elena whispered that she didn't think any of them ……………………………………………………………… , yet somehow they all did.
items.answer: were going to live to tell the tale
acceptedAnswers: were going to live to tell the tale

items.sentence:
He’s determined to come out on top in the end, though!
laugh He’d much rather ………………………………………………………………… in the end, though!

exercises.title: C: Cloze (San Francisco Earthquake)
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Natural disasters have tested the resilience of cities throughout history, but rarely in modern times (21) ...... a single earthquake caused such devastating destruction as the one that struck San Francisco in 1906.
San Francisco, (22) ...... lies along the San Andreas Fault on the west coast of the United States, was hit by a catastrophic earthquake on 18th April 1906.

(23) ...... only was the initial tremor (24) ...... violent that buildings collapsed across the city, but the fires that raged in its aftermath are also recognised as (25) ...... the most destructive urban blazes (26) ...... recorded in American history.
Over three thousand people lost their lives in the disaster, many (27) ...... in the fires that burned for three days and nights.
The earthquake had an enormous impact on the (28) ...... infrastructure, prompting a complete overhaul of building regulations throughout California.
The fires destroyed a remarkable (29) ...... of the city's buildings, leaving more than half the population without homes.
Only after several weeks of intensive relief work (30) ...... authorities manage to restore order and begin the process of reconstruction.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: D: One Word for Three Sentences
exercises.title: D: One Word for Three Sentences
exercises.instructions: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.
exercises.studentInstruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

exercises.title: F: Verb Forms
exercises.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in brackets.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: It’s high time ...... (we / redecorate) the office.
...... (I / take) your advice earlier, I wouldn’t be in this difficult position now.
I do wish ...... (you / mention) it to Sandra, but you have so it’s too late now.
...... (live) abroad for five years, she returned home with a much broader view of the world.
Maria told me ...... (she / attend) the workshop several times that year already, so she felt quite confident.
If ...... (I / drive) any faster, I’d risk attracting the attention of the police!
It’s time for ...... (you / apologise) to your sister, young man.
Would you sooner ...... (I / book) a table for us, or would you prefer to call yourself?
...... (you / be) to receive confirmation next week that your application was successful, how would you react?
...... (ask) to present in front of the entire board of directors made me incredibly nervous.

exercises.title: G: Phrasal Verbs
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I hear Marcus has taken ...... wearing a hat to work – apparently it’s become his signature look.
She may have lost the first round, but I wouldn’t write ...... her chances of winning the championship just yet.
We need to spread ...... along the riverbank and search the whole area thoroughly.
The original safety proposals were watered ...... by the industry representatives to make them more commercially acceptable.
We were so sorry to hear about your grandfather's stroke. He is going to pull ...... , isn't he?
You really mustn’t let the older students push you ...... !
I’m worried about Patrick – he’s barely eating anything and looks like he’s wasting ...... .
It didn’t take us long to cotton ...... to the fact that the new manager had a very short temper.
How exactly do you plan to drum ...... enough interest in the fundraising event?
I give ...... ! Just tell me what the answer is.

exercises.title: H: Word Formation (Sentence Level)
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Why do manufacturers put so many ............................ in soft drinks? **ADD**
Could you ................................... the image slightly so we can read the small print? **LARGE**
The judge stated that it was not the court's place to ............................ blame between the two parties. **PORTION**
The scandal caused ............................ damage to the politician's public image. **REPAIR**
The new exhibition hall is far more ............................ than the previous one, with capacity for twice as many visitors. **SPACE**
All allegations against the professor were found to be completely ............................ . **SUBSTANCE**
Your bank statement provides a record of all financial ............................ carried out during the previous month. **ACT**
Although the floor appears perfectly level, this impression is ............................ – it actually slopes by almost two degrees. **ILLUSION**
Millions of families in the developing world continue to experience severe financial ............................ . **HARD**
Being the hardest known natural substance, diamond is virtually ............................ under normal conditions. **DESTROY**

exercises.title: K: Cloze (Dialogue)
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap. Use the words in the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap. Use the words in the box.

items.sentence: Hey, Jamie! Long time no (101) ...... !
I know! I’ve had so much (102) ...... lately – work has been absolutely relentless.

exercises.title: L: Vocabulary Matching
exercises.instructions: Match a word from the box with each phrase. You will not need one of the words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Match a word from the box with each phrase. You will not need one of the words.

items.sentence: a medical expert called in for a specialist opinion
a person who intimidates others who are less powerful than they are
a person who relies on another for financial support

items.sentenceBefore: We need to give this issue the
items.sentenceAfter: priority if we are to resolve it before the deadline.
items.originalSentence: We need to give this issue the **tallest / highest** priority if we are to resolve it before the deadline.
items.completedSentence: We need to give this issue the highest priority if we are to resolve it before the deadline.

items.sentenceBefore: At long
items.sentenceAfter: , the planning committee has approved the new community centre.
items.originalSentence: At long **last / end**, the planning committee has approved the new community centre.
items.completedSentence: At long last , the planning committee has approved the new community centre.

items.sentenceBefore: Good dentists are a bit thin on the
items.sentenceAfter: in this part of the city.
items.originalSentence: Good dentists are a bit thin on the **land / ground** in this part of the city.
items.completedSentence: Good dentists are a bit thin on the ground in this part of the city.

items.sentenceBefore: The union representative is planning to
items.sentenceAfter: a new wage proposal at tomorrow’s meeting.
items.originalSentence: The union representative is planning to **table / chair** a new wage proposal at tomorrow’s meeting.
items.completedSentence: The union representative is planning to table a new wage proposal at tomorrow’s meeting.

items.sentenceBefore: Investing in green spaces greatly improves the
items.originalSentence: Investing in green spaces greatly improves the **quality / standard** of life for city residents.
items.completedSentence: Investing in green spaces greatly improves the quality of life for city residents.

items.sentenceBefore: It’s just a
items.sentenceAfter: feeling, but I’d say she’s not being entirely honest with us.
items.originalSentence: It’s just a **stomach / gut** feeling, but I’d say she’s not being entirely honest with us.
items.completedSentence: It’s just a gut feeling, but I’d say she’s not being entirely honest with us.

items.sentenceBefore: The organisation has agreed to
items.sentenceAfter: the group permission to hold a peaceful demonstration.
items.originalSentence: The organisation has agreed to **consent / grant** the group permission to hold a peaceful demonstration.
items.completedSentence: The organisation has agreed to grant the group permission to hold a peaceful demonstration.

items.sentenceBefore: Many young people begin experimenting with alcohol as a result of peer
items.originalSentence: Many young people begin experimenting with alcohol as a result of peer **pressure / force**.
items.completedSentence: Many young people begin experimenting with alcohol as a result of peer pressure .

items.sentenceBefore: Have you seen the
items.sentenceAfter: new coffee machine they’ve installed in the staffroom?
items.originalSentence: Have you seen the **model / brand** new coffee machine they’ve installed in the staffroom?
items.completedSentence: Have you seen the brand new coffee machine they’ve installed in the staffroom?

items.sentenceBefore: Research suggests the traditional
items.sentenceAfter: family has changed considerably over the past half-century.
items.originalSentence: Research suggests the traditional **nuclear / atomic** family has changed considerably over the past half-century.
items.completedSentence: Research suggests the traditional nuclear family has changed considerably over the past half-century.

exercises.title: N: Grammar (Fill in the gaps)
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap. Use the words in the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap. Use the words in the box.

items.sentence: Sarah doesn’t enjoy early morning meetings and ……………………… do I, to be honest.
If you don’t have …………………………… money for a taxi, I can always lend you some.
If …………………………… the supermarket had stayed open just a bit later!
……………………… as we wanted to accept their offer, the location simply wasn’t right for us.
Even …………………………… you feel fully recovered by Monday, I would still recommend taking one more day off.
The building project went ahead …………………………… widespread opposition from local residents.
The sequel is …………………………… near as gripping as the original film.
I do find …………………………… remarkable that some people manage to learn several languages to a high level.
Stop talking as …………………………… you know exactly how events are going to unfold.
……………………… did she realise that a surprise party was being planned right under her nose.
